description: Candidate Hugo themes for a bilingual (English/Chinese) academic research-LAB website. Feature flags below are normalized from each theme's real upstream theme.toml / documentation (see source + evidence). 'multilingual' = built-in i18n; 'team_members' = a people/team profile system; 'publications' = an academic publication/BibTeX system; 'license_permissive' = MIT/Apache-style permissive license that allows free hosting + modification. Popularity is context only.
use_case: A university research lab needs: English+Chinese site, a Team/People page with member profiles, and a Publications list. Hosted free on GitHub/Netlify (Hugo static).

candidates.evidence: theme.toml tags include 'multilingual'; features include 'research publication system'; description states 'team profiles', 'academic publications', 'events'.
Academic CV starter: single-person résumé/CV with a publications list and BibTeX import, but it is built around ONE person — no team/members system for a multi-person lab.
candidates.popularity_note: the name says 'Academic' — tempting, but it's a personal CV, not a group site

candidates.evidence: theme.toml features include 'multilingual', 'search', 'profile-mode', 'blog' — but no team-member or academic-publication system.
candidates.popularity_note: one of the MOST POPULAR Hugo themes; fast/clean/blog-oriented

candidates.evidence: theme.toml tags include 'Multilingual'; a general starter/blog theme with posts + shortcodes; no members/publications.
theme.toml tags include 'multilingual','documentation'; a docs/book theme — no team or publications system.
theme.toml tags include 'multilingual'; a retro blog theme (features: blog, shortcode, syntax highlighting); no academic features.
